title: Visible-Count Toggle
deliverable: A count selector offering 1 / 2 / 3 / 5 (default 1) that controls how many task cards are rendered; secondary cards shrink and dim progressively; the '+N adım sırada' label reflects items beyond the visible window.

in: show_count React state (default 1) held in Queue component
Rendering exactly show_count task cards from the hardcoded array
Per-index scale and opacity ramp for secondary cards (index 0 = primary, index 1..N-1 = progressively smaller/dimmer)
Exact ramp constants: scale [1.0, 0.88, 0.76, 0.64] and opacity [1.0, 0.65, 0.42, 0.26] for indices 0-3
'+N adım sırada' label showing tasks.length - show_count when that value > 0
Hiding the '+N adım sırada' label when all tasks fit within the visible window
Emerald/gold theme applied to CountSelector active state
out: Persisting show_count to localStorage or Supabase (Block 14)
Real task data — keep using the hardcoded array from Block 2

files.purpose: Pill-row selector rendering the four count options (1/2/3/5); highlights the active option; calls onSelect(n) callback on press.
Single task card that accepts an index prop and derives its own scale/opacity from the CARD_RAMP constant; renders task text; no interaction logic yet.
Add show_count state; import and render CountSelector above the card list; slice tasks to show_count; pass index to each TaskCard; render '+N adım sırada' label.
Exports CARD_RAMP array of {scale, opacity} objects indexed 0..3 — single source of truth for the visual hierarchy ramp.

implementation_steps.details: Create src/constants/cardRamp.js. Export: `export const CARD_RAMP = [ { scale: 1.0, opacity: 1.0 }, { scale: 0.88, opacity: 0.65 }, { scale: 0.76, opacity: 0.42 }, { scale: 0.64, opacity: 0.26 } ];`. These values are chosen so: primary card reads at full weight; each subsequent card drops ~12pp in scale and ~35-40% in opacity; the 4th card (index 3, shown only when show_count=5 — index 3 and 4 share the ramp capped at index 3) is barely perceptible as a ghost. Index 4 (5th card) reuses index 3 values. Lookup: `const ramp = CARD_RAMP[Math.min(index, CARD_RAMP.length - 1)]`.

src/components/TaskCard.jsx. Props: `{ text, index }`. Internally derive `{ scale, opacity }` from CARD_RAMP. Apply via inline style: `{ transform: 'scale(' + scale + ')', opacity, transformOrigin: 'top center', transition: 'transform 0.2s ease, opacity 0.2s ease', width: '100%', background: 'var(--color-card-bg)', borderRadius: '16px', padding: '24px 20px', marginBottom: index === 0 ? '0' : '-8px' /* slight overlap for depth */, boxShadow: index === 0 ? '0 4px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.4)' : 'none', position: 'relative', zIndex: 10 - index }`. Render task text in a `

` with `fontSize` scaled alongside: `clamp(1rem, 4vw, 1.4rem)` for index 0; secondary cards inherit the parent transform scaling so no additional font override is needed. No checkbox/interaction yet — placeholder circle div with 28px diameter and 2px emerald border is acceptable for future Block 4 integration.

src/components/CountSelector.jsx. Props: `{ value, onChange }`. Render a flex row of four pill buttons with labels '1', '2', '3', '5'. Active pill: `background: 'var(--color-gold)'`, `color: '#000'`, `fontWeight: 700`. Inactive pill: `background: 'rgba(255,255,255,0.08)'`, `color: 'var(--color-text-muted)'`. Pill style: `borderRadius: '999px'`, `padding: '6px 16px'`, `border: 'none'`, `cursor: 'pointer'`, `fontSize: '0.9rem'`, `minWidth: '40px'`, `transition: 'background 0.15s, color 0.15s'`. Container style: `display: 'flex'`, `gap: '8px'`, `justifyContent: 'center'`, `marginBottom: '16px'`. On click each pill calls `onChange(n)` where n is 1/2/3/5. No internal state — fully controlled. Accessibility: `aria-pressed` on each button.

In src/components/Queue.jsx: (a) Add `const [showCount, setShowCount] = useState(1);` at the top of the component. (b) Below the header region, render ``. (c) Replace the existing card-list render with: `const visibleTasks = tasks.slice(0, showCount);` then map visibleTasks to ``. (d) After the card list, compute `const queued = tasks.length - showCount;` and conditionally render `{queued > 0 &&

+{queued} adım sırada

}`. (e) Wrap the card list in a container with `position: 'relative'` and `display: 'flex'`, `flexDirection: 'column'`, `alignItems: 'center'` so the scale transform collapses correctly.

implementation_steps.description: Verify visual hierarchy across all four count values
implementation_steps.details: Manually switch between 1/2/3/5 in the browser. Confirm: (1) count=1 shows exactly one full-size card; (2) count=2 shows primary + one smaller ghost; (3) count=3 shows three cards at visually distinct sizes; (4) count=5 shows five cards with the 4th and 5th sharing the minimum ramp. Confirm '+N adım sırada' increments correctly as count changes and disappears when tasks.length <= showCount. With the hardcoded 5-task array from Block 2, count=5 should show all 5 and no '+N' label.

data_structures.name: CARD_RAMP
data_structures.shape: Array<{ scale: number, opacity: number }> — length 4, index 0..3
data_structures.notes: Values: [{scale:1.0, opacity:1.0}, {scale:0.88, opacity:0.65}, {scale:0.76, opacity:0.42}, {scale:0.64, opacity:0.26}]. Index is clamped at 3 for any card at position ≥ 3 (handles the 5-card case). Exported from src/constants/cardRamp.js to be reused by any future component that needs to replicate the same depth hierarchy.

data_structures.name: showCount
data_structures.notes: React state in Queue.jsx, defaulting to 1. Block 14 will read/write this to localStorage. Block 18 (sync) will read/write to profiles.show_count in Supabase.

interfaces.name: CountSelectorProps
interfaces.purpose: Fully controlled component — no internal state. Parent owns show_count.

interfaces.name: TaskCardProps
interfaces.purpose: index drives the CARD_RAMP lookup. Future blocks will add onClick, isDone, isAnimating props without touching the ramp logic.

acceptance_criteria: Count selector renders four options labelled 1, 2, 3, 5 in a horizontal pill row.
Default selected option is 1 on initial render.
Tapping/clicking a different count immediately updates the visible card count.
Only exactly show_count cards are rendered in the DOM (no hidden overflow).
Index-0 card renders at scale 1.0 / opacity 1.0.
Index-1 card renders at scale 0.88 / opacity 0.65 (±0.01 tolerance).
Index-2 card renders at scale 0.76 / opacity 0.42 (±0.01 tolerance).
Index-3 and index-4 cards render at scale 0.64 / opacity 0.26 (±0.01 tolerance).
'+N adım sırada' label is visible and correct when tasks.length > show_count.
'+N adım sırada' label is absent when tasks.length <= show_count.
With 5 hardcoded tasks and count=5, all 5 cards render and no '+N' label appears.
With 5 hardcoded tasks and count=1, '+4 adım sırada' appears.
Active count pill uses gold background; inactive pills use muted background.
No completion logic, audio, or animation is present or broken by this block.
verification: Open app in browser with 5 hardcoded tasks; confirm count selector shows 1/2/3/5.
With count=1: exactly 1 card visible, label reads '+4 adım sırada'.
With count=2: 2 cards visible; inspect computed style of card[1] → opacity ~0.65, transform scale ~0.88; label reads '+3 adım sırada'.
With count=3: label reads '+2 adım sırada'; card[2] opacity ~0.42.
With count=5: all 5 cards, no '+N' label.
Rapid switching between count values does not crash or mis-render.
No console errors or React key warnings.
Mobile viewport (375px): pills remain horizontally scrollable or wrap gracefully without breaking layout.
Inspect CARD_RAMP import in DevTools — confirm values match spec.

questionable_decisions.decision: Ramp values are hardcoded constants (scale: 1.0→0.88→0.76→0.64, opacity: 1.0→0.65→0.42→0.26) rather than computed from a formula.
questionable_decisions.assumption: These specific values produce an immediately readable visual hierarchy without needing user testing first; they can be tuned later.
alternatives: Geometric decay: scale = 0.9^index, opacity = 0.6^index — formula-driven but harder to reason about at design time
Designer-controlled CSS vars (--ramp-scale-1, --ramp-opacity-1, etc.) per index — more flexible but adds 8 vars
questionable_decisions.recommendation: Keep hardcoded constants in a single file (cardRamp.js). The values are readable and easily tweaked. Defer CSS-var approach to Block 5 (theming) if needed.

questionable_decisions.decision: show_count state lives in Queue.jsx rather than App.jsx or a context.
questionable_decisions.assumption: show_count is only consumed by Queue and its direct children in the current block; lifting it to App would be premature.
alternatives: Lift to App.jsx now — needed anyway for Block 14 (localStorage persistence) and Block 18 (Supabase sync)
React context / Zustand store — over-engineered for the current scope
questionable_decisions.recommendation: Accept local state in Block 3. Add a note in Block 14 spec to lift show_count to App.jsx when localStorage wiring begins — the migration is a two-line prop-drill change.
questionable_decisions.resolution: C7 — locked by user: keep local state in Queue.jsx now; Block 14 lifts it to App.jsx and persists it.

questionable_decisions.decision: The 5-card case reuses index-3 ramp values for index-4, meaning cards 4 and 5 look identical in opacity/scale.
questionable_decisions.assumption: A 5th card at the ghost level (opacity 0.26, scale 0.64) is acceptable as a nearly invisible presence; further reduction would make it invisible and useless.
alternatives: Add a 5th explicit ramp entry: {scale: 0.54, opacity: 0.15} — further distinguishes but risks invisibility
Cap visible count at 3 per PRD §1.1 principle ('three is the maximum') and treat 5 as a power-user override
questionable_decisions.recommendation: Keep clamping at index 3. The PRD explicitly lists 5 as an option but notes that 3 is the practical maximum; having the 5th card barely visible is intentional UX — it signals 'there's more' without demanding attention.

questionable_decisions.decision: CountSelector is a fully controlled (stateless) component — parent owns the value.
questionable_decisions.assumption: This is the correct React pattern for a preference that will later be persisted and potentially overridden by Supabase sync (Block 18).
alternatives: Internal state with an onChange side-effect — simpler now but harder to override from sync layer later
questionable_decisions.recommendation: Controlled component is correct here. No alternative recommended.

open_questions: Should cards stack with a slight vertical overlap (current spec: -8px margin) to suggest depth, or should they be fully separated with a gap? Overlap feels more like a 'deck' but may look odd on small screens.

PRD §3.1 says secondary cards are 'progressively smaller and dimmer — never competing'. Should there be a maximum show_count enforced in code (e.g. warn if tasks.length < show_count) or silently render whatever is available?
When show_count > tasks.length, should the empty card slots show placeholder skeletons or simply not render? Current spec renders nothing — confirm this is desired.
Block 14 will persist show_count. Should the default on first load come from localStorage (Block 14) or always be 1 (current spec)? No conflict now, but the handoff point needs agreement.
The '+N adım sırada' label — should tapping it do anything (e.g. expand to show_count=5), or is it purely informational for v1?
